title: Acanthophyllum luteum
intro: Acanthophyllum luteum is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae, native to Iran and the surrounding regions. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving symptoms of colds and flu, as well as promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Acanthophyllum luteum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Acanthophyllum luteum is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
